title: Eva 30 New Angel from Sneak Peak
display_title: Eva 30 New Angel from Sneak Peak
wikitext:
[]: Eva 3.0: New Angel from Sneak Peak             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:47 am So, any theories/thoughts about the new Angel introduced in the first few minutes of Rebuild 3.0? Here's what we know it can do: *Fires A.T. Field-propelled rockets that can pierce other A.T. Fields and make a screaming noise as they fly. *Initially appears as a nondescript box-like object on the black cross holding Eva-01. Then it rapidly unfolds into several metal strings. *For its first attack, the Angel forms a "ring of light" similar to Armisael and unleashes a white cross-laser that damages Eva-02. *It then forms an atom-like shape and blasts Eva-02 with explosions.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:48 am I'm not so sure the box itself was Angel. Asuka sure got surprised when it turned out to have pattern blue

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:50 am It definitely looks man-made from my end, at least partially. Someone said in the "First Seven Minutes" thread that the laser attack is created by using the reflective ribbons to redirect sunlight, which sounds like a pretty good explanation to me. It seems like it was put inside the box-like object and attached to Unit-01's capsule as some sort of defense mechanism.

[]:             Quote Post by Kodaemon » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:51 am The disc form has parts that look suspiciously like Eva shoulder fins.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:57 am The Proposal had a ribbon angel. Which I think later got turned into Sahaquiel, but it's nice to see the design make a return.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Fri Nov 16, 2012 11:58 am riffraff11235 wrote: It definitely looks man-made from my end, at least partially.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Nov 16, 2012 12:00 pm Shinoyami65 wrote: Could it have been modified like Tunniel? It doesn't seem manmade completely, judging by the presence of a core and the traditional cross-shaped explosion+rainbows, as well as its inexplicable attacks. But I wouldn't put it past Seele to modifiy the Angel, allowing them to control it, like the pseudo-entry plug on Tunniel . I wonder where they dug it up from though, because Angels aren't very easy to capture (as demonstrated by Sandalphon).

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Fri Nov 16, 2012 12:08 pm It would be pretty cool to actually see someone "pilot" an Angel with an entry plug. But it probably won't happen, after the Tunniel incident. The Space Angel could be controlled by remote, due to its mechanical additions.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Nov 16, 2012 12:09 pm Kodaemon wrote: The disc form has parts that look suspiciously like Eva shoulder fins.

[]:             Quote Post by Jayfive » Fri Nov 16, 2012 1:56 pm My vote is not an Angel is the truest sense. Defence mechanism developed from angel bio-technology (i.e. Tunniel)

[]:             Quote Post by Kodaemon » Fri Nov 16, 2012 2:09 pm Jayfive wrote: My vote is not an Angel is the truest sense. Defence mechanism developed from angel bio-technology (i.e. Tunniel)

[]:             Quote Post by Stan » Fri Nov 16, 2012 3:15 pm I dont know if I want to believe that its a man made angel. Why you make it so confusing Anno?

[]:             Quote Post by Kodaemon » Fri Nov 16, 2012 3:29 pm Watching the clip in slo-mo, yep, there's more of them. I'm calling them Droniels . As for why - why not? They've already cyberized and weaponized angels as Evas , they also have the dummy system now.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Nov 16, 2012 3:42 pm ^ I'd call them Funniels instead.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Nov 16, 2012 3:45 pm yeah, Funniels they are Gotta respect funnel tradition

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Fri Nov 16, 2012 7:24 pm Kodaemon wrote: I'm thinking this as well. Also, seems to me like there are at least TWO of those things - the first one has four fins and gets destroyed by Mari, then a second one, with two fins, emerges from the sarcophagus.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Nov 16, 2012 7:58 pm Shinoyami65 wrote: I thought the little Funniels were being launched by the larger one as a form of attack, but now I see that wouldn't work, as it was camoflaged at the time...They may have been launched from Earth by Seele to attack Asuka and Mari. Intriguingly, the missile-Funniels also identify as "Pattern Blue" if I'm understanding the radio jargon right...I think they should be treated as a subspecies of Angel.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Sat Nov 17, 2012 12:09 am Judging from what I've read so far Funniel and the missiles are referred to collectively as the Eleventh Angel. So they probably have an Ireul-like hive mind, and are thus treated as a single enitity.

[]:             Quote Post by shiava » Sat Nov 17, 2012 5:43 am welp, i posted this reply on another post, but i guess it belongs here i don't know if someone has pointed this out yet. As far as i remember, Seele made some experiments on tunniel, Kaji said that the angel got "dissected" and we know that it had a dummy plug on it my theory is that those flying disk aren't a "new" angel, these are small (and modified) parts of our old friend tunniel. Seele took those pieces and made something completely new out of them (a defenssive mechanism). It should be considered as an "artificial" angel, not an "original" angel son of adam. again, this is just my theory

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Sat Nov 17, 2012 7:19 am That would make sense, except that the smaller missile-Funniels don't resemble "parts" of Tunniel at all. To me they look like weaponised versions of the larger Funniel's core/hub. I think they might be parts of the big Funniel or based on it, but they don't have that much in common with Tunniel. Edit: Funniels have been named as Mk. 04A (missile drones) and Mk. 04B (big metal thread-Armisael thing). The names suggest they might be prototypes for the Mark 06 seen in 2.0, or possibly remnants of the destroyed Eva-04. Some people are still calling the Funniels the Eleventh Angel, though, so they may be considered Angels despite their origins. You can see the names in the subbed first 6 minutes of 3.0: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NnigpyCYHOc&feature=youtube_gdata_player
